title: User equipment, radio network node and methods performed therein
abstract: Embodiments herein relates to a method performed by a UE (10) for handling communication in a wireless communications network. The UE (10) transmits a first indication in a PHR of a serving cell to a radio network node (12), wherein the first 5 indication indicates presence of a first type of PH of a first carrier in the PHR of the serving cell, and wherein the first type is referring to an uplink RS transmission power. The PHR of the serving cell further comprises a second indication indicating presence of a PH of a second type of a second carrier of the serving cell, wherein the second type is referring to a user data transmission power.10(Fig. 3)
claims:
Claims 1. A method performed by a user equipment, UE, (10) for handling communication in a wireless communications network, the method comprising transmitting (603) a first indication in a power headroom report, PHR, of a serving cell to a radio network node, wherein the first indication indicates presence of a first type of power headroom, PH, of a first carrier in the PHR of the serving cell, and wherein the first type is referring to an uplink reference signal, RS, transmission power and wherein the PHR of the serving cell further comprises a second indication indicating presence of a PH of a second type of a second carrier of the serving cell, wherein the second type is referring to a user data transmission power. 2. The method according to claim 1 , further comprising receiving (600) configuration data indicating whether the UE (10) is enabled to report the type of PH or not. 3. The method according to any of the claims 1-2, wherein the first indication and the second indication are in a preconfigured order in the PHR of the serving cell. 4. The method according to any of the claims 1-3, wherein the first indication and/or the second indication are comprised in a subheader of medium access control, MAC, control element. 5. The method according to any of the claims 1-4, wherein the first carrier is a new radio uplink carrier and the second carrier is a supplementary uplink, SUL, carrier, or vice versa. 6. The method according to any of the claims 1-5, wherein the first type is a type 3 referring to a sounding reference signal, SRS, transmission power and the second type is a type 1 referring to the user data transmission power. 7. A method performed by a radio network node (12) for handling communication in a wireless communications network (1), the method comprising 

 receiving (702), from a user equipment, UE, (10) a first indication in a power headroom report, PHR, of a serving cell, wherein the first indication indicates presence of a first type of power headroom, PH, of a first carrier in the PHR of the serving cell and wherein the first type is referring to an uplink reference signal, RS, transmission power, and wherein the PHR of the serving cell further comprises a second indication indicating presence of a PH of a second type of a second carrier of the serving cell, wherein the second type is referring to a user data transmission power; and estimating (703) a transmission power status of the UE (10) based on the first and/or second type of the PH in the PHR of the serving cell. 8. The method according to claim 7, further comprising using (704) the estimated transmission power status in modulation coding scheme, MCS, selection, rank adaption and/or power control at the radio network node of respective carrier. 9. The method according to any of the claims 7-8, further comprising transmitting (701) configuration data indicating whether the UE (10) is enabled to report the type of PH or not. 10. The method according to any of the claims 7-9, wherein the first indication and the second indication are in a preconfigured order in the PHR of the serving cell. 11. The method according to any of the claims 7-10, wherein the first indication and/or the second indication are comprised in a subheader of medium access control, MAC, control element. 12. The method according to any of the claims 7-11 , wherein the first carrier is a new radio uplink carrier and the second carrier is a supplementary uplink, SUL, carrier. 13. The method according to any of the claims 7-12, wherein the first type is a type 3 referring to a sounding reference signal, SRS, transmission power and the second type is a type 1 referring to the user data transmission power. 14. A user equipment, UE, (10) for handling communication in a wireless communications network, wherein the UE (10) is configured to: transmit a power headroom report, PHR, of a serving cell to a radio network node wherein the PHR of the serving cell comprises a first indication indicating presence of a first type of power headroom, PH, of a first carrier in the PHR of the serving cell, and wherein the first type is referring to an uplink reference signal, RS, transmission power and wherein the PHR of the serving cell further comprises a second indication indicating presence of a PH of a second type of a second carrier of the serving cell, wherein the second type is referring to a user data transmission power. 15. The UE (10) according to claim 14, wherein the UE (10) is further configured to receive configuration data indicating whether the UE (10) is enabled to report the type of PH or not. 16. The UE (10) according to any of the claims 14-15, wherein the first indication and the second indication are in a preconfigured order in the PHR of the serving cell. 17. The UE (10) according to any of the claims 14-16, wherein the first indication and/or the second indication are comprised in a subheader of medium access control, MAC, control element. 18. The UE (10) according to any of the claims 14-17, wherein the first carrier is a new radio uplink carrier and the second carrier is a supplementary uplink, SUL, carrier. 19. The UE (10) according to any of the claims 14-18, wherein the first type is a type 3 referring to a sounding reference signal, SRS, transmission power and the second type is a type 1 referring to the user data transmission power. 20. A radio network node (12) for handling communication in a wireless communications network, wherein the radio network node (12) is configured to receive from a user equipment, UE, (10) a first indication in a power headroom report, PHR, of a serving cell, wherein the first indication indicates presence of a first type of power headroom, PH, of a first carrier in the PHR of 

 the serving cell and wherein the first type is referring to an uplink reference signal, RS, transmission power, and wherein the PHR of the serving cell further comprises a second indication indicating presence of a PH of a second type of a second carrier of the serving cell, wherein the second type is referring to a user data transmission power; and estimate a transmission power status of the UE (10) based on the first and/or second type of the PH in the PHR of the serving cell. 21. The radio network node (12) according to claim 20, wherein the radio network node (12) is configured to use the estimated transmission power status in modulation coding scheme, MCS, selection, rank adaption and/or power control at the radio network node of respective carrier. 22. The radio network node (12) according to any of the claims 20-21 , wherein the radio network node (12) is further configured to transmit configuration data indicating whether the UE (10) is enabled to report the type of PH or not. 23. The radio network node (12) according to any of the claims 20-22, wherein the first indication and the second indication are in a preconfigured order in the PHR of the serving cell. 24. The radio network node (12) according to any of the claims 20-23, wherein the first indication and/or the second indication are comprised in a subheader of medium access control, MAC, control element. 25. The radio network node (12) according to any of the claims 20-24, wherein the first carrier is a new radio uplink carrier and the second carrier is a supplementary uplink, SUL, carrier. 26. The radio network node (12) according to any of the claims 20-25, wherein the first type is a type 3 referring to a sounding reference signal, SRS, transmission power and the second type is a type 1 referring to the user data transmission power. 27. A computer program product comprising instructions, which, when executed on at least one processor, cause the at least one processor to carry out the method according to any of the claims 1-13, as performed by the radio network node (12) or the UE (10), respectively. 28. A computer-readable storage medium, having stored thereon a computer program product comprising instructions which, when executed on at least one processor, cause the at least one processor to carry out the method according to any of the claims 1-13, as performed by the radio network node (12) or the UE (10), respectively.
